machinery_genes.quote: ClpV is crucial for a functional T6SS and interacts through its N-terminal domain with the VipA/VipB complex that is composed of two conserved and essential members of T6SS... VipA/VipB form tubular, cogwheel-like structures
machinery_genes.note: Bonemann 2008/2009, V. cholerae. VipA = TssB = VCA0107 (locus tag from Zheng 2011, which writes 'the products of VCA107 (VipA) and VCA108 (VipB)'). Sheath inner subunit; the TssA paper calls VipAB 'also known as TssBC'.

machinery_genes.quote: VipA/VipB form tubular, cogwheel-like structures that are converted by a threading activity of ClpV into small complexes
machinery_genes.note: Bonemann 2008/2009, V. cholerae. VipB = TssC = VCA0108 (Zheng 2011: 'VCA108 (VipB)'). Sheath outer subunit; VipAB = TssBC per TssA paper.

machinery_genes.quote: In most T6SSs, hemolysin co-regulated protein (Hcp) and valine-glycine repeat protein G (VgrG) are exported by T6SS. They are proposed to be the extracellular components of T6SS apparatus
machinery_genes.note: Hcp = TssD, the secreted inner tube. This Aux1 instance's Hcp is Hcp-1 (VC1415); Zheng 2011 Table 1 lists 'VC1415 (hcp-1)'. Counted as machinery per the brief (secreted tube).

machinery_genes.role: trimeric spike / tube tip (secreted; Aux1 spike loading the effector)
machinery_genes.quote: VgrG-1, which functions as both a T6SS structural element and an effector protein, contains a C-terminal domain with strong homology to the actin cross-linking domain (ACD)
machinery_genes.note: VgrG-1 = TssI = VC1416, encoded in the Aux1 (VC1415-VC1421) cluster that carries this instance's effector VC_1418. Listed as machinery for its structural spike role (the VgrG core, not the fused ACD effector domain, which is excluded).

machinery_genes.role: trimeric spike (Aux2 cluster; required for assembly/killing)
machinery_genes.quote: we found that VgrG-2 is the only VgrG protein individually essential for secretion as well as killing of amoebae and bacteria
machinery_genes.note: VgrG-2 = VCA0018, second VgrG spike. Included as a core spike component of the shared V. cholerae T6SS machinery (the three VgrGs interact to form the functional spike).

machinery_genes.quote: VCA0121 and valine-glycine repeat protein G 3 (VgrG-3), are not essential for Hcp expression, secretion or bacterial virulence
machinery_genes.note: VgrG-3 = VCA0123, the VgrG encoded in the main T6SS cluster. Third spike protein; structural VgrG core listed as machinery (its peptidoglycan-hydrolase effector domain would be an effector, excluded here).

machinery_genes.quote: proteins from the PAAR (proline-alanine-alanine-arginine) repeat superfamily form a sharp conical extension on the VgrG spike... We demonstrate that PAAR proteins are essential for T6SS-mediated secretion and target cell killing by Vibrio cholerae and Acinetobacter baylyi
machinery_genes.note: Shneider/Mekalanos 2013, demonstrated essential in V. cholerae. PAAR caps the VgrG spike. Multiple PAAR genes exist (e.g. VCA0105 PAAR structure 4JIV); listed as the PAAR machinery family rather than a single locus.

machinery_genes.quote: twelve (VipA, VipB, VCA0109-VCA0115, ClpV, VCA0119, and VasK) are essential for Hcp secretion and bacterial virulence, and thus likely function as structural components of the apparatus
machinery_genes.note: ClpV = TssH = VCA0116 (Zheng 2011: 'deletion of clpV (VCA116)'). AAA+ ATPase; Bonemann 2009 shows it remodels the VipA/VipB sheath. Counted per brief.

machinery_genes.quote: VipA, VipB, VasF, VasH, ClpV, VasK, and VgrG-3 have been characterized by genetic knockout... VasH is a regulator and VipA, VipB, VasF, ClpV and VasK are T6SS structural components essential for Hcp secretion
machinery_genes.note: VasF = VCA0115, a DotU/TssL-family inner-membrane complex component (the TssA paper: 'MC proteins TssM and TssL are homologues of the type IV secretion system proteins IcmF and DotU, respectively'). Named structural component here.

machinery_genes.quote: VipA, VipB, VasF, ClpV and VasK are T6SS structural components essential for Hcp secretion
machinery_genes.note: VasK = IcmF = TssM = VCA0120 (Zheng 2011 abstract lists VasK among the twelve structural components; the membrane-complex IcmF/TssM identity from the TssA paper). Largest inner-membrane component anchoring the trans-envelope complex.

machinery_genes.quote: the outer-membrane lipoprotein TssJ recruits the inner-membrane protein TssM that binds to another inner membrane partner TssL, forming a 5-fold trans-envelope MC composed of 15 copies of TssJ and 10 copies each of TssM and TssL
machinery_genes.note: Santin/Cianfanelli/Basler 2020 name TssJ as the V. cholerae OM lipoprotein of the membrane complex (TssJLM). Distinct from VasF/VasK (TssL/TssM) which carry V. cholerae vas names; TssJ used here as the lipoprotein has no separate vas synonym in the cited work.

machinery_genes.quote: The BP comprises 6 copies of the TssEFGK wedge complex wrapping around a trimeric VgrG spike in the center
machinery_genes.note: Baseplate wedge component named for the V. cholerae T6SS in Santin et al 2020 (TssEFGK).

machinery_genes.quote: TssF interacting with VgrG and the sheath VipAB (also known as TssBC)
machinery_genes.note: Baseplate wedge component, V. cholerae T6SS (Santin et al 2020).

machinery_genes.quote: The BP comprises 6 copies of the TssEFGK wedge complex wrapping around a trimeric VgrG spike in the center
machinery_genes.note: Baseplate wedge component, V. cholerae T6SS (Santin et al 2020). Named within the TssEFGK wedge.

machinery_genes.quote: with TssK serving as the main connector to the TssML inner membrane complex
machinery_genes.note: Baseplate connector linking BP to the TssML inner-membrane complex, V. cholerae T6SS (Santin et al 2020).

machinery_genes.quote: The T6SS cluster of [V. cholerae] encodes two ImpA_N domain proteins, TssA and TagA, which belong to the TsaC and TsmA classes, respectively
machinery_genes.note: Santin et al 2020 explicitly name TssA as encoded in the V. cholerae T6SS cluster; facilitates sheath-tube polymerization. Accessory but core to assembly.

machinery_genes.quote: The T6SS cluster of [V. cholerae] encodes two ImpA_N domain proteins, TssA and TagA, which belong to the TsaC and TsmA classes, respectively
machinery_genes.note: Santin et al 2020 name TagA as the second ImpA_N protein in the V. cholerae T6SS cluster; terminates sheath extension. Accessory machinery.

notes: System: the Vibrio cholerae T6SS, discovered by Pukatzki et al 2006 (the founding 'vas' paper) in non-O1/non-O139 strain V52 and conserved in the seventh-pandemic reference strain N16961. The V. cholerae T6SS is encoded by ONE main cluster (VCA0107-VCA0124 on chromosome 2) plus TWO auxiliary clusters: Aux1 = VC1415-VC1421 (chromosome 1; contains hcp-1=VC1415, vgrG-1=VC1416, and effector/immunity genes VC1417-VC1421) and Aux2 = VCA0017-VCA0021 (hcp-2, vgrG-2). This instance is Aux1, whose curated effector is VC_1418. STRAIN TRANSFER: the gene-by-gene apparatus characterisation (Zheng/Ho/Mekalanos 2011) used strain V52 but reports results against the N16961 locus-tag numbering (VCA0107-VCA0123, VC1415/VC1416), so names transfer directly to N16961; V52 and N16961 T6SS loci are essentially identical. The apparatus (membrane complex, baseplate, sheath, ATPase) lives in the MAIN cluster and is shared by all three clusters; the Aux1-specific secreted parts are Hcp-1 (VC1415) and VgrG-1 (VC1416), both listed. Coverage: sheath VipAB(TssBC), tube Hcp(TssD), spike VgrG-1/2/3(TssI), PAAR tip, ATPase ClpV(TssH), inner-membrane complex VasF(TssL/DotU) + VasK(TssM/IcmF), OM lipoprotein TssJ, baseplate TssEFGK, and assembly accessories TssA + TagA. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: delivered toxin effectors and their immunity proteins (e.g. VgrG-1 actin-cross-linking ACD domain, VgrG-3 cell-wall hydrolase domain, VasX=VCA0020, the Aux1 effector VC_1418 and immunity genes VC1417/VC1419-1421); VasH (VCA0117) and VCA0122 are transcriptional regulators (VasH kept as one flagged non-structural row, see its note). LOW-CONFIDENCE / NOTES: Zheng 2011 confirms VCA0109-VCA0114 and VCA0119 as essential 'structure protein[s]' but does not assign them individual Tss letters, so those locus tags are not split into separate named rows here; the canonical baseplate (TssEFGK) and membrane-complex (TssJLM) names are taken from Santin et al 2020, which names them generically as V. cholerae T6SS components without per-locus-tag mapping. Multiple PAAR genes exist in N16961 (e.g. VCA0105); PAAR listed as a family, not a single locus. All quotes verbatim from the cited PubMed/PMC texts. According to PubMed.
